clay_red.description: Red modeling clay, used to represent hydrogen atoms in molecular models.

clay_white.description: White modeling clay, used to represent oxygen atoms in molecular models.

clay_black.description: Black modeling clay, used to represent carbon atoms in molecular models.

clay_green.description: Green modeling clay, used to represent chlorine atoms in molecular models.

toothpick.description: Wooden toothpick, used to represent chemical bonds between atoms in molecular models.

H2_model.description: Molecular model of H₂: two red clay spheres connected by one toothpick.

Cl2_model.description: Molecular model of Cl₂: two green clay spheres connected by one toothpick.

H2O_model.description: Molecular model of H₂O: one white clay sphere (O) connected to two red clay spheres (H) by two toothpicks at 105°.

CO2_model.description: Molecular model of CO₂: one black clay sphere (C) connected linearly to two white clay spheres (O) by two toothpicks (180°).

NH3_model.description: Molecular model of NH₃: one white clay sphere (N) connected to three red clay spheres (H) by three toothpicks in a trigonal pyramidal geometry.

CH4_model.description: Molecular model of CH₄: one black clay sphere (C) connected to four red clay spheres (H) by four toothpicks in a tetrahedral geometry.

Sodium.description: A soft, silvery-white, highly reactive metal. Freshly cut surfaces are shiny but quickly tarnish in air.

Kerosene.description: A clear, colorless to pale yellow liquid hydrocarbon mixture used as a solvent and fuel.

Sodium Oxide.description: A white solid formed when sodium reacts with oxygen in air.

mixed_solution.description: Aqueous solution of iron(III) nitrate, pale yellow color.

Alum_Powder.description: Alum powder, potassium aluminum sulfate dodecahydrate, commonly used as a coagulant in water purification.

Flocculated_Impurities.description: Aggregated and precipitated impurities formed after alum-induced flocculation.

silver_ammonia_solution.description: Colorless, clear aqueous solution of diamminesilver(I) hydroxide, commonly called Tollens' reagent.

glucose_solution_10_percent.description: 10% (w/v) aqueous solution of D-glucose, colorless and clear.

silver_mirror.description: Metallic silver deposited on the inner wall of the test tube, forming a mirror-like layer.

CuCl2_solution_25pct.description: Aqueous solution of copper(II) chloride at 25% mass fraction; blue-green color.

Cu(s).description: Solid copper metal; reddish color, deposited at cathode.

Cl2(g).description: Chlorine gas; pale green, evolved at anode.

sample_liquid.description: A turbid or impure water sample containing suspended solids and/or other impurities; appears cloudy or colored depending on contamination.

filtered_sample_liquid.description: The filtrate obtained after filtering the sample liquid; contains fewer suspended solids and appears clearer than the original sample.

residue_on_filter_paper.description: Solid impurities or suspended particles retained on the filter paper after filtration; may appear as a gray or brown residue.

dilute_CuSO4.description: Dilute aqueous solution of copper(II) sulfate, pale blue.

Cu.description: Solid copper, reddish-brown metal deposited on the iron wire.

glucose.description: Glucose, a simple sugar produced by hydrolysis of starch.
